stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 2020, Carlos Mendoza and colleagues in Spain and Portugal studied the effect of ambient noise on swallows’ success in feeding chicks at urban nests.
  • They found that as noise levels increased, the parent birds completed fewer successful feedings per hour.
  • In quiet conditions, swallows averaged about 22 successful feedings per hour.
  • In moderate traffic noise, the average dropped to roughly 14 feedings per hour.
  • In heavy traffic noise, swallows averaged only around 7 feedings per hour.
stem: The student wants to summarize the study. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Carlos Mendoza’s 2020 study of swallows’ success in feeding chicks at urban nests showed that as ambient noise levels increased, the parent birds’ feeding rate decreased.
For a 2020 study, researchers from Spain and Portugal collected data on swallows’ chick-feeding attempts in different noise conditions.
Carlos Mendoza and his colleagues wanted to know the extent to which ambient noise affected swallows’ success in feeding chicks at urban nests, so they conducted a study.
Knowing that swallows feed their chicks frequently at urban nests, Carlos Mendoza studied the birds’ feeding rate, which was only around 7 feedings per hour in some conditions.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence effectively summarizes the study, noting who conducted it, when it was conducted, and what its results showed: that the swallows’ feeding rate decreased as ambient noise levels increased.

Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence presents the methodology, it fails to summarize the study as a whole. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence presents the aim of the study, it fails to summarize the study as a whole. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence indicates what Mendoza studied, it fails to mention a key factor: the effect of noise.
